# Professional Language Guidelines

## Goal

- Ensure responses use plain, professional, tester-friendly wording.

## Input

- Draft prompts, test steps, and expected results text.
- [SOURCE_TEXT]
- [TARGET_FILE]
- [CONTEXT]
## Constraints

- Do not use emojis or emoticons.
- Write from the tester point of view.
- Avoid vague phrases and ambiguous wording.
- Avoid semicolons and overly long statements.

## Expected Output

- Clear, direct, professional content suitable for QA documentation.
- Consistent terminology and actionable phrasing.
- Format: bullet list
